name: Bogarra — circular from Peñas de San Pedro
name_original: Bogarra — circular desde Peñas de San Pedro
description: Challenging 59.9-mile road cycling loop from Peñas de San Pedro, featuring 4941 feet of elevation gain and varied terrain.
region_intro: Road cycling around Peñas De San Pedro features a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes and varied terrain, offering challenging ascents and rewarding descents. The region includes rocky outcrops and natural areas, providing diverse backdrops for road cycling routes. Distances range from moderate to long, with routes often incorporating substantial climbs. Best road cycling routes around Peñas De San Pedro The most popular road cycling route is Bogarra — circular from Peñas de San Pedro, a 59.9 miles (96.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 47 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 4,900 feet (1,500 meters) of elevation gain, offering sustained climbs and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Peñas de San Pedro – Peñas de San Pedro loop from Argamasón, a moderate 37.9 miles (60.9 km) path. This loop route provides a balanced ride with over 1,300 feet (400 meters) of elevation gain through the local landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Peñas de San Pedro – Viewpoint of Bogarra loop from Peñas de San Pedro, a 53.3 miles (85.7 km) trail leading through scenic viewpoints, often completed in about 4 hours 22 minutes. Road cycling around Peñas De San Pedro is defined by rocky terrain, significant elevation changes, and open landscapes. The network offers options for both moderate and challenging ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 170 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Peñas De San Pedro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level for road cycling routes around Peñas De San Pedro?
A: Road cycling around Peñas De San Pedro is characterized by significant elevation changes and varied terrain, making most routes moderate to difficult. There are no easy routes listed in this guide, with 6 moderate and 5 difficult routes available, offering sustained climbs and rewarding descents.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly or family-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: Given the region's characteristics of significant elevation changes and challenging ascents, there are no easy or specifically beginner-friendly routes listed in this guide. Most routes are moderate to difficult, which may not be suitable for young families or novice road cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the road cycling routes?
A: The terrain around Peñas De San Pedro is varied, featuring rocky outcrops, natural areas, and open landscapes. You can expect significant elevation changes, with routes often incorporating substantial climbs and varied surfaces that provide a diverse backdrop for your ride.

Q: Are there any loop routes available for road cycling?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1285976" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas de San Pedro – Peñas de San Pedro loop from Argamasón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 37.9-mile path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31463527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas de San Pedro – Viewpoint of Bogarra loop from Peñas de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; is a 53.3-mile trail leading through scenic viewpoints.

Q: What are some of the longer or more challenging road cycling routes?
A: For a challenging ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1167753118/bogarra-circular-from-penas-de-san-pedro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bogarra — circular from Peñas de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 59.9-mile route with over 4,900 feet of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Pesebre to Cilleruelo – Alcaraz loop from Casas de Lázaro&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 70 miles and features nearly 6,400 feet of ascent.

Q: What attractions or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you might pass by interesting points such as the settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6571470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Salobral&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6785177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casas de Lázaro&lt;/a&gt;. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5623714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Peñas de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5623849" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Our Lady of Hope&lt;/a&gt; are also notable landmarks in the region.

Q: Are there any routes that offer particularly scenic viewpoints?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31463527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas de San Pedro – Viewpoint of Bogarra loop from Peñas de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; is specifically highlighted for leading through scenic viewpoints. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40308104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador La Rodea Grande – Los Infiernos Viewpoint loop from Alcadozo&lt;/a&gt; suggests routes with impressive vistas.

Q: When is the best season for road cycling in Peñas De San Pedro?
A: To avoid the intense summer heat, the best seasons for road cycling in Peñas De San Pedro are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these months, temperatures are generally milder, and the weather is more conducive to long rides, allowing you to fully enjoy the region's challenging terrain.

Q: What should I pack for a road cycling trip in this region?
A: Given the significant elevation changes and varied terrain, it's advisable to pack layers of clothing, including a windbreaker or light jacket for descents. Essential items include plenty of water, nutrition, a repair kit, and sun protection. Always check the weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the routes?
A: While specific cafes are not listed for every route, many routes pass through or near small towns and villages where you can typically find local establishments for refreshments. Planning your route to include stops in towns like Peñas de San Pedro itself or other settlements can ensure you have access to food and water.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Peñas De San Pedro area?
A: There are 11 road cycling routes detailed in this guide for the Peñas De San Pedro area, offering a range of moderate to difficult options for cyclists looking to explore the region's unique landscape.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Peñas De San Pedro?
A: Over 170 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Peñas De San Pedro's varied terrain. They often praise the challenging ascents, rewarding descents, and the diverse backdrops provided by the rocky outcrops and natural areas, making for a fulfilling cycling experience.
tips: Incredible views of Bogarra from the top of the viewpoint, totally worth the effort.

If you pass through this town I recommend you visit its castle, which is located on top of the Peña del Castillo. It is an old fortress, important during the Middle Ages due to its strategic location. Among the buildings in the town of Peñas de San Pedro, the parish church of Nuestra Señora de la Esperanza stands out.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Bogarra — circular from Peñas de San Pedro cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered a difficult road ride, demanding very good fitness due to its significant length of approximately 96.4 kilometers and an elevation gain of about 1506 meters. It's designed for experienced road cyclists seeking a substantial challenge.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is primarily well-paved, typical for a racebike route. However, be prepared for some segments that might be unpaved and difficult to ride, potentially requiring you to push your bike. The route winds through scenic areas, including rocky landscapes and old village streets.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given its difficulty and length, the estimated duration for completing this route is around 4 hours and 48 minutes of active cycling time. However, due to its demanding nature, many cyclists may take longer, especially when accounting for breaks and enjoying the scenery.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning views of the Sierra del Segura, including the rocky mass of Pico Padrastro. You'll pass through areas with orchards and charming old villages. Notable highlights include the Viewpoint of Bogarra and the historic Peñas de San Pedro castle. In the vicinity of Bogarra, you can also find natural attractions like the Ruta de las Fuentes and El Batán with its impressive waterfalls.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking facilities are not detailed, Bogarra and Peñas de San Pedro are likely starting points. Look for designated parking areas within these towns, especially near the main access roads, to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in this region, making the long and challenging ride more strenuous. Winters can bring colder temperatures and potentially adverse weather conditions.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebiking on roads, which typically isn't suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists. If you wish to bring a dog, it would be best to explore dedicated walking or hiking trails in the region.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for cycling in Sierra de Alcaraz?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for road cycling on public roads within the Sierra de Alcaraz region. However, always respect local regulations, especially if you venture onto private land or protected natural areas.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other official trails, including the CIMA AB06 Loma de la Albarda, Variante 2, Camino Histórico Paterna-Catalmerejos, Los Catalmerejos-Cortijo de Tortas, and the Vereda de la Huesa.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: As the route passes through villages like Bogarra and Peñas de San Pedro, you should find opportunities for cafes, restaurants, or small shops to refuel and hydrate. It's always wise to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially given the route's length and difficulty.

faqs.question: Is accommodation available for cyclists near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Bogarra and Peñas de San Pedro, as well as surrounding towns in the Sierra de Alcaraz, offer various accommodation options ranging from guesthouses to small hotels that can cater to cyclists. It's advisable to book in advance, especially during peak seasons.
